properties.name: text
properties.value: Up the quarry road, at the mouth of the deep cut: a second set of bootprints beside Tam's. Human, even-spaced, unhurried. Whoever made them walked away calm.

properties.name: text
properties.value: Tam slept rough in the ruins to save wages. Under a loose hearthstone: his savings, and a letter to his sister. He found something worth money, and a gentleman offered to buy his silence.

properties.name: text
properties.value: You stand at Coombs's grave and the Listening opens. One word, in his gap-toothed voice, patient as a man with all the time there is: PAPER.

properties.name: text
properties.value: Low voices below the stone, taking a count that never finishes. Eleven, someone keeps starting. Eleven. Eleven.

objects.name: a pale light by the creek
a light up the quarry road

properties.name: text
properties.value: A cast-iron marker at the head of the National Road. The great pike west begins here at Cumberland and runs to the Ohio country, the first road the young republic ever built with its own hands. Men leave from this stone and do not come back east.

properties.name: text
properties.value: Where Braddock's Road climbs northwest toward the fort hill. General Braddock cut this trace in 1755 and died on it, and a young Colonel Washington buried him in the roadbed so the wagons would grind the grave flat and hide it from the French. The wagons still pass over him. Everyone here knows, and no one marks the spot.
